observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan   consider the passage of a resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor to execute a deed of donation of the PPE currently used by the DepEd.

We also recommended that thereafter, the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to take up in the books of accounts the following proposed adjusting journal entry to fairly present the balances of PPE and Government Equity accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to prepare the necessary documents outlining the information relative to the removed structures of road networks and furnish the same to the Municipal Accountant which will serve as her basis in determining the appropriate carrying amount of replaced parts of local road networks and other infrastructure assets to be derecognized from the books in order to fairly and reliably present these accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the proposed adjusting journal entry to recognize the shares of the DPWH and OBO from collections of Building Permit Fees:

We also recommended that the Municipality create and maintain a Trust Fund Account for Building Permit Fees and other charges as required under Item 2.1 of the DILG and DPWH JMC No. 1 dated July 4, 2014 and henceforth remit the corresponding shares of the DPWH and OBO.

We recommended that henceforth,

5.6.1	the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to observe closer monitoring and evaluation of all ongoing infrastructure projects may not be compromised and ensure completion of the project within the specified contract duration so that benefits that could be derived therefrom are immediately realized;
5.6.2	 if delays are incurred in the completion of a project, implement calibrated measures provided in GPPB Circular No. 03-2019 prior to termination in part or in whole, of the work that has not been completed and accepted under a contract; and
5.6.3	impose administrative sanctions, if warranted, in accordance with Section 69 of the IRR of RA No. 9184, now governed by Rule XXI of the IRR or RA No. 12009, against erring suppliers.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of charging expenditures against the 20% DF which are not allowable to be charge out of the fund, otherwise, the transactions shall be disallowed in audit.  Henceforth, see to it that the 20% DF be optimally utilized for the intended purpose to help achieve the desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of the municipality as prescribed under the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2020-01 dated November 4, 2020.

We recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Mayor:

7.5.1	require the Municipal Treasurer and all officials and employees to observe strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and liquidate fully all cash advances at the end of the year or as soon as the purpose for which it was granted has been served; and
7.5.2	instruct the Municipal Accountant not to process claims for additional cash to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made to avoid misappropriation and possible loss of government funds.

We recommended that cash advances be made only for payment of salaries and wages, honoraria and for other petty operating expenses.  Claims above ₱15,000.00 for each transaction should be made through checks.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Chairman to furnish the Auditor a copy of all contracts together with the required supporting documents within five (5) working days after execution/perfection in order for the Audit Team to perform timely review of the same so that deficiencies, if any, could be communicated to management for immediate and appropriate action, and possible sanctions to employees concerned for unjustified failure to comply will be accordingly avoided.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:

10.7.1	adequately observe the deadline set for the submission of trial balances and other financial reports as required under Section 7.2.1a of COA Circular 2009-006; and
10.7.2	submit the revenue and disbursement documents to the Audit Team until the 10th day of the following month.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor appoint a permanent Municipal Budget Officer for a more efficient delivery of services to the constituents.  In addition, designate an Agency Inspector on the procurement of goods other than the Municipal Accountant and those involved in the disbursements and recording/processing financial transactions to enhance the accuracy and reliability of financial information/data.

We recommended that henceforth, the Sangguniang Bayan immediately appropriate funds from RA 7171 and 8240 for the speedy implementation of the programs and projects.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
12.12.1	instruct the BAC to immediately start the procurement process of all the programs/projects funded out of the RA 7171 funds which were not yet subjected to public bidding, and
12.12.2	require all concerned Department Heads to facilitate the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order to give the tobacco farmers who worked hard for the generation of the funds the early enjoyment of benefits they rightfully deserve.  In addition, we recommended immediate implementation to enable the municipal government to avail of lesser costs for these programs/projects and in order that more benefits could be derived from these funds considering the unending price increase of all commodities.

We recommended that the municipality fully implement its Solid Waste Management Plan and the pertinent provisions of Republic Act. No. 9003 in order to safeguard the public health from substantial present and potential hazardous wastes and attain a safe, clean and green environment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer intensify the collection of real property taxes and conduct massive tax campaign in all the barangays.  Furthermore, seek the cooperation of barangay officials since they have also shares on the collection of real property taxes. Thus, additional income would be collected to fund other priority programs and projects of the municipality which are beneficial to its constituent.

We recommended that the Municipal School Board monitor the collections under SEF and utilization of the same that would address the purpose enumerated in RA 7160 or the Local Government Code of the Philippines.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that henceforth require the:

a. Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Accounting Office a schedule of unreleased checks at the end of every month or quarter, and


Municipal Accountant to restore the cash equivalent of all unreleased checks by debiting Cash in Bank – LCCA and crediting the appropriate payable/liability accounts supported with a working paper at the end of the month or quarter and by preparing a JEV at the end of the year based on the submitted schedule from the Municipal Treasurer.  In addition, in the ensuing year, prepare a JEV to reverse the entries made covering the restoration in the previous year.

7.9.1 	immediately work out for the issuance of red plates by the LTO for the  nine vehicles without plate numbers  and install the same  in front and at the rear of every vehicle/truck;
7.9.2 	work out for the conversion of the four vehicles with private plates into red plates, and
7.9.3 	cause for the proper markings of all vehicles owned by the municipality as prescribed in COA Circular No. 77-61 in order to prevent unauthorized or improper use thereof.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit collections intact with the authorized depository bank whenever she goes to the bank for the encashment of check/s to avoid the accumulation of huge cash in her possession thereby minimizing the risk of possible loss, misuse or misappropriation of government funds and at the same time enable the municipality to earn additional income in the form of interest on bank deposits.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
9.4.1 	submit justification/explanation on the aforementioned lapses;
9.4.2 	require all the collectors that at the close of each business day, they accomplish all the data in the RCD, affix their signatures therein and turn over all their collections to the Municipal Treasurer, and
9.4.3 	verify the correctness of all entries in the RCD prepared by every collector before receiving the collections and  affixing her signature therein.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
11.6.1 	instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare immediately the disbursement vouchers for the remittance of the trust collections due to the Barangays in the total amount of  ₱224,126.33;
11.6.2 	require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer that henceforth, release the share of each barangay on realty taxes on a quarterly basis within five days after the end of each quarter to enable the latter to utilize the funds for its operations, and
11.6.3 	require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to make fund transfer of the RPT shares of the barangays from the bank account of the municipality to their corresponding bank account for safer, faster and easier remittance of shares and to avoid issuance of checks.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require:
13.6.1 	the Municipal Engineer to instruct all contractors to see to it that henceforth, all laborers are provided with complete and appropriate PPEs and cause for the installation of warning devices at the project site whenever necessary in order not to compromise the health and safety of the workers and the public, and indicate the actual percentage of completion of the pay items in the SWA, and
13.6.2 	the Municipal Accountant to carefully scrutinize the supporting documents of  disbursement vouchers and determine with certainty the amount  due to the contractors in order not to unduly benefit the latter at the expense of the municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed to inform the Auditor in writing on the posting of infrastructure projects billboards within ten days after the award of the contract and comply with the submission of monthly report of on-going and completed contracts as required under COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 20, 2013 in order to promote transparency in the implementation of infrastructure projects and to ensure that the right of the people to information on matters of public concern is addressed.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to stop the practice of splitting contracts just to avoid public bidding.  Require the BAC to conduct competitive public bidding for all procurements and resort to alternative methods of procurement only when the conditions provided in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 are met.  Moreover, the BAC see to it that the most advantageous price is obtained in every procurement.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
16.7.1	require the BAC through the BAC TWG to see to it that performance securities have validity periods in accordance with the IRR of RA 9184 so that potential loss of government funds would be avoided, and
16.7.2 	instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that surety bonds submitted by the contractors/suppliers are properly reviewed before any payment is made in order to have assurance that the contractor will perform its obligation and to protect the government from incurring losses due to any defects that may occur.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
17.8.1	instruct the Municipal Engineer to require the contractors to prepare the As-Built Plans of the 13 infrastructure projects; verify the correctness of the same and submit to the Office of the Auditor,  and
17.8.2 	require the  Municipal Accountant not to process claims for final payment of infrastructure projects unless these are supported with As-Built-Plans prepared by the contractors, among others, in order to ascertain the  validity and propriety of the transactions.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
18.8.1 	require the one in-charge in the issuance of Driver’s Trip Tickets to put serial number in chronological order on all Driver’s Trip Tickets and see to it that the same are approved by the authorized official before these are issued to the drivers;
18.8.2 	instruct all drivers   not to drive municipal vehicles/trucks/ heavy equipment without duly approved Driver’s Trip Ticket and    fill out the corresponding data required therein before and after the trip, and
18.8.3 	require the GSO Designate and the Municipal Accountant to assist the drivers in the preparation of the Monthly Report of Official Travels    and instruct them to prepare the Report of Fuel Consumption for all vehicles/heavy equipment owned by the municipality and submit the same to the Auditor within the first ten days of the succeeding month.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Monthly Report, furnish the same to the LDRRMO for the latter to reconcile with his record after which submit to the COA Auditor the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund not later than the 15th day after the end of each month so that errors/deficiencies could be communicated to Management on time for appropriate corrective action.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require:
20.5.1 	the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the cash allocated for Trust Liability – DRRMF from the bank account of General Fund to the bank account of Trust Fund in order to facilitate accounting and monitoring  of  said fund and avoid possible confusion on the usage of check booklet to be used , and
20.5.2 	the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary accounting entries after the bank transfer in order to reflect the correct balances of Cash in Bank for each fund  and minimize  if not to eliminate  the balances of the Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds accounts

We recommended and Management agreed to require the GAD Focal Point Officer to submit the GAD Plan and Budget to the COA Audit Team within five days from receipt of the DILG-endorsed GPB, and the GAD Accomplishment Report duly received by the DILG within five days from the end of January of the ensuing year.   Hence, deficiencies if any, could be communicated to Management on time for appropriate corrective action.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the:
22.8.1 	Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to coordinate with the GSIS with regard to the unremitted premium contributions of the newly hired employees, salary differentials of promoted employees and salary increases, and
22.8.2 	Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to cause the immediate remittance of all GSIS premium contributions in compliance with the relevant provisions of RA No. 8291  in order not to deprive the concerned municipal officials and employees of their GSIS privileges and benefits and avoid the imposition of penalties.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary documents for the application of bond for the Revenue Collection Clerk II and the GSO Designate and pay the corresponding bond premiums in order to protect the interest of the government.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to submit the quarterly financial statements on time in order to maximize usefulness of the financial information and would enable Management to make timely corrective measures for any errors, omissions and deficiencies discovered through the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit monthly bank reconciliation statements in order to establish the accuracy and reliability of the Cash in Bank balances as required in COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
26.7.1 	instruct the Accounting Personnel to sign the “prepared by” portion of the JEV;
26.7.2 	require the Municipal Accountant to review the accounting journal entries reflected in the JEV  and  affix her signature in the “approved by” portion before posting such entries in the corresponding journals, and
26.7.3 	require the Municipal Accountant to properly accomplish the GLs by providing all the necessary information or details pertaining to the account such as the Particulars and References in order to facilitate the verification of   the accuracy of data posted therein which shall be forwarded to the trial balances and the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
27.5.1 	require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the 79 cancelled checks, and
27.5.2 	instruct the Municipal Treasurer that henceforth, attach all the cancelled checks to the Report of Checks Issued and submit the same together with the cancelled checks to the Municipal Accountant who shall submit these documents to the Office of the Auditor for verification and inspection, thus deficiencies, if any could be immediately communicated to Management for corrective action thereof.

We recommended and management agreed that henceforth, the “Requesting Official” portion of the CAFOA and Box A of the disbursement voucher of Special Education Fund be signed by the district supervisor or his/her authorized representative to enable the said official to monitor disbursements and accomplishments and most importantly, to institute corrective/remedial measures to ensure full attainment of targets.

We recommended and Management agreed to require employees/officials availing any loans to secure a clearance, from the Municipal Accountant, as to the monthly net take home pay to make sure that the same is enough to cover any deductible loan amortization from the monthly salaries/wages.  Henceforth, strictly implement the yearly provision in the GAA regarding the mandatory monthly net take home pay of P5,000.00 in order to boost the employees’ morale   which would ultimately redound to a more efficient and effective delivery of   services to the general public.

We recommended and the Sangguniang Bayan Agreed that henceforth, immediately appropriate funds from RA 7171 and 8240 for the speedy implementation of the programs and projects.

We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
30.16.1	instruct the BAC to immediately start the procurement process of all the programs/projects funded out of the RA 7171 funds which were not yet subjected to public bidding, and
30.16.2 	require all concerned Department Heads to facilitate the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order to give the tobacco farmers who worked hard for the generation of the funds the early enjoyment of benefits they rightfully deserve.  In addition, we recommended immediate implementation to enable the municipal government to avail of lesser costs for these programs/projects and in order that more benefits could be derived from these funds considering the unending price increase of all commodities.

We recommended that Management instruct Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant exert their best efforts to locate the foregoing discrepancies and cause the reconciliation of the balances of PPE accounts in the ledgers with that of the RPCPPE as basis for the preparation of adjusting journal entry, if necessary, to reflect the correct balances.
observations.managementAction: Management is continually exerting best efforts to locate the foregoing discrepancies and cause the reconciliation of the balances of PPE accounts in the ledgers with that of the RPCPPE as basis for the preparation of adjusting journal entry, if necessary, to reflect the correct balances in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Verification of the financial statements and report of physical inventory revealed a discrepancy of P3,580,723.21. Said difference was decreased from the previous balance of P17,151,628.01 to P3,580,723.21 due to the efforts exerted by management in reconciling the same. Reiterated in Observation No. 1.6 of the report, page 39.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Require all Department Heads strict compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. The Municipal Accountant to meticulously scrutinize all disbursement vouchers submitted for processing and ensure that these are completely supported with the required documents before effecting payment thereof to ascertain the validity, legality, propriety and correctness of the financial transactions. b. As regards the foregoing transactions, instruct the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents and address the deficiencies noted to determine the validity, propriety and correctness of all the transactions and in compliance with the abovementioned provisions of law and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is continually checking the completeness of supporting documents but due to voluminous transactions, some might have been misplaced or attached to the copy retained at the Accounting Office. The lacking documents were already submitted to the Auditor.
observations.reason: Post-audit checking revealed that there were still various transactions that were not supported with complete documents but due to voluminous transactions, some supporting documents might have been misplaced or attached to the copy retained at the Accounting Office. The Audit Team noted that some lacking documents were later submitted to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer strictly comply with the provisions set forth in COA Circular No. 2013-004. Accomplish and update signboards/billboards to enable the public to see and read relevant information for them to assess if PPAs are fairly implemented and justifiably completed which in turn promotes good governance through transparency.
observations.managementAction: Engineer claimed that they were doing their best to comply on the Audit Team’s recommendation but due to constraint in manpower, they might not able to update all project billboards that were still not updated showing the status of the ongoing construction.
observations.reason: Review of contracts and disbursement records disclosed that there were some project billboards that were still not updated showing the status of the ongoing construction due to manpower constraints although some were updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC Chairman and Head of BAC Secretariat strict adherence with the provisions provided in the 2016 Revised IRR of 9184 by observing the following: a. the BAC members to meticulously conduct the process of post-qualification to ensure that the awarded bidder qualifies, having posted and submitted all the requirements and conditions as specified in the Bidding Documents and to guarantee their capability and competence to undertake and to government projects; b. the BAC Secretariat as the main support unit of the BAC conduct their functions and responsibilities in adherence to 14.1 of the Revised 2016 IRR of RA 9184 and make sure that all documents relative to the BAC activities are properly accomplished in order not to cast doubt on the validity and propriety of the procurement activities; and c. the BAC Secretariat to notify in writing all bidders who submitted their bid proposals in harmony with Section 25.7 of the 2016 IRR to ensure transparency.
observations.managementAction: The Chairperson claimed that the BAC has doing their best efforts to improve and adhere with all the requirements of the procurement law. However, due to manpower and time constraints, some important documentation might have been overlooked. The Head of the BAC Secretariat claimed that due to voluminous transactions/works, some supporting documents and details might have been misplaced or attached to the copy retained at the BAC Office. The BAC Secretariat already sending notices to all bidders who submitted their bid proposals.
observations.reason: Post-audit and review of contracts showed several activities in the post-qualification process that were not observed; some required supporting documents were missing or misplaced due to voluminous transactions and manpower constraints. Management assured to submit lacking documents and address deficiencies.

observations.recommendation:
1.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1.8.1 Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
1.8.1.1 prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to reclassify and record the
properties in their appropriate accounts by debiting Other Structure, Flood
Control System and Water Supply System the amounts of P994,441.21,
P4,995,707.83 and P998,299.44 respectively and crediting Road Networks
and CIP – Infrastructure Assets the amounts of P230,085.47 and
P6,758,363.01 accordingly;
1.8.1.2 prepare journal entry to take up in the books of accounts the
unrecorded cost of Construction of Two Storey Multi-Purpose Building
project at Ul-oling, Pob. Norte, Gregorio del Pilar, Ilocos Sur with contract
cost of P2,497,351.00 by debiting Building and crediting Government Equity;
1.8.1.3 verify the records on the details of projects added to the CIP-Infrastructure
Assets account and those that were transferred to their appropriate accounts
to locate for the discrepancy amounting to P962,492.85, in order to present
the balance of this account accurately; and
1.8.1.4 provide in the books of accounts the impairment losses of unserviceable
properties with carrying cost totaling P556,268.05 by debiting Impairment
Loss–PPE account and crediting the corresponding Accumulated Impairment
Loss-PPE by the amount equivalent to their book value/carrying cost.

1.8.2 Instruct the Property Officer, the Head of Inventory Committee and the
Municipal Accountant to adhere with the guidelines and procedures set forth
under COA Circular 2020-06 dated January 31, 2020 for the conduct of
physical count of properties; recognition of PPE items found at station;
disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items; and for the one-time
cleansing of PPE account balances, in order to reflect more reliable and
accurate balances of PPE accounts, hence to convey precise information useful
in decision making and accountability for these properties.

2.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant strict compliance with the provision of Section 50 of the
MNGAS to record/close the CIP account to the appropriate PPE accounts
of the properties as soon as the projects are completed in order to present
their balances in the quarterly financial reports accurately.

3.7.1 Direct the Municipal Accountant to observe the Perpetual Inventory
Method of accounting for inventories, maintain supplies ledger cards
and reconcile her records regularly with the records maintained by
the GSO-Designate; and
3.7.2 Require the GSO-Designate to maintain stock cards and
prepare/submit weekly the Summary of Supplies and Materials
Issued (SSMI) together with the original copy of the Requisition
Issue Slip (RIS) to the Municipal Accounting Office as basis in
recording the issuances of inventory items.
3.7.3 Thus, may result in accurate and reliable balances of inventories in
the Financial Statements and in the Report of the Physical Count of
Inventories.

4.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant strict compliance on the abovementioned regulations
pertaining to the submission of the monthly and quarterly trial balances,
including the supporting SAOB, journals, ledgers and supporting schedules
to facilitate review and verification thereon, and deficiencies if any, may be
immediately communicated to management, for the latter’s consideration.

5.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Property
Officer notify the Audit Team of all deliveries of procured items within
twenty-four (24) hours from the acceptance so that the Auditor could
conduct the timely inspection/validation of same and communicate results
thereof for Management’s consideration.

6.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
6.7.1 Instruct the Property Officer to gather all the unserviceable
properties and prepare the IIRUP thereon and submit the same to
the COA Auditor for inspection to facilitate immediate disposal of
the same to avoid further decline in their realizable value, hence,
additional possible revenue from the disposal may be generated;
6.7.2 Require the Property Officer to submit a copy of the IIRUP to the
Municipal Accountant as her basis in recording the impairment
losses of these properties in order to present fairly the balances of
the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
6.7.3 Create a disposal committee who will take charge in the disposal of
unserviceable properties adhering with the process of disposal as
prescribed in COA Circular 92-386 on the Rules and Regulations on
Supply and Property Management in local government units and the
guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts
balances.

7.16 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
7.16.1 Require the Municipal Accountant and concerned officials strict
compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and pertinent
provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012;
7.16.2 Require the concerned Department Heads/Officials to submit the
lacking documents to the Municipal Accountant who will verify the
completeness and accuracy thereof and subsequently submit the
same to the Auditor in order for the latter to verify the validity,
propriety and correctness of these documents and compliance of said
transactions with the abovementioned provisions of law and
regulations; and
7.16.3 Moving forward, support all disbursement vouchers with complete
and accurate documents before effecting payment thereof to
ascertain the validity, propriety and correctness of the transactions.

8.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Engineer to:
8.6.1 require the contractors to prepare the SWA for verification by the
Municipal Engineer and the same shall be used in the conduct of
inspection and preparation of the report thereof and the basis for the
progress billings for the ongoing and completed projects; and
8.6.2 submit immediately said lacking documents to the Auditor through
the Municipal Accountant in order to verify the validity and
propriety of these transactions and to facilitate immediate request
for inspection thereof.

9.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
9.9.1 Instruct the General Services Officer to assign knowledgeable
personnel to check the details or review the propriety and
completeness of information of the trip tickets prior to official trips
and to regularly submit Monthly Report of Official Travels and
Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Auditor thru the
Municipal Accountant for purposes of verification on the
reasonableness of fuel consumption; and
9.9.2 Require the Municipal Accountant to review and verify the
completeness of documentary requirements for claims against
municipal funds before affixing his signature on Box A of the
disbursement voucher to ascertain the validity and propriety of
disbursements made.

10.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to intensify the collection of Real Property and Special Education
Taxes. Send notices of collection to the delinquent taxpayers to remind
them of their obligation. Likewise, conduct massive tax campaign in all the
barangays and offer tax amnesty in order to motivate delinquent taxpayers
to avail of the offer.

11.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Engineer and MPDO to prepare the corresponding program of works of
identified projects prior to endorsement for budget allocation and require
also the Municipal Budget Officer not to include the funding of projects
without the approved budget estimates which is based on the approved
program of work to avoid the allocation of excessive allotment, hence, other
PPAs of the Municipality may likewise be funded.

12.6 We recommended that Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer
intensify on-site supervision and evaluation of performance on the
abovementioned projects to resolve any problems and bottlenecks and to
adapt the calibrated actions in response to delays in the implementation of
infrastructure projects provided in Item 4.2 of GPPB Circular No. 03-2019
and Item 7 and 8 of Annex E of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184 to ensure
completion of succeeding projects in accordance with the contract duration,
thus constituents/beneficiaries would avail of the benefits from the
completed projects.

13.2.3 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to
meticulously conduct post-qualification to determine whether the
bidder concerned complied with and is responsive to all the
requirements and conditions as specified in the bidding documents,
hence the ability of the bidder to undertake the contract is ensured.

13.3.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to
carefully verify/check the computation of submitted NFCC to
ensure that the bidder has the capacity to finance and implement
the bid contract.

13.4 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC
Secretariat to post the NOA in the PhilGEPs on the prescribed
period as required in Sections 37.1.6 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA
No. 9184, in order to promote transparency and efficiency in the
conduct of procurement in the government.

13.5.3 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to:
13.5.3.1 immediately submit the abstract/printout copy of the posting
of advertisement, award and notice to proceed in the
PhilGEPS, to ascertain that these were actually posted in the
PhilGEPS website on the prescribed period in adherence with
the posting requirement of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No.
9184; and
13.5.3.2 post all subsequent bid opportunities in the PhilGEPS website
to conform with the commitment of the government in the
promotion of good governance and transparency through the
dissemination of bid opportunities to the public for the
information of interested bidders, pertinent non-government
organizations, as well as its constituents.

13.6.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC
Secretariat to ensure that all relevant communications/documents
issued to the bidder particularly the NOA and NTP are duly affixed
with the date of receipt by the concerned bidder, hence ascertaining
that the date of the perfection of the contract and its effectivity
conforms with the recommended timeline as prescribed in Sections
37.2.1 and 37.4.1 and Annex C of the 2016 Revised IRR of the RA
No. 9184, thus, incurrence of delays in the delivery and
implementation of the project could be immediately determined.

13.7.4 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to
ensure that the Performance Security are duly/properly dated in
64
compliance with Sections 37.2.1, 37.2.3 and 39.1 of the 2016 Revised
IRR of RA No. 9184 to guarantee the faithful performance by the
winning bidder of its obligations as stipulated in the contract.

13.8.2 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to:
13.8.2.1 ensure that all reports and relevant documents pertaining to
the conduct of bidding proceedings are duly filled out and
properly dated; and
13.8.2.2 submit the corrected and duly dated/filled out
reports/documents of the abovementioned procurement
activities, such as:
• Resolution of the BAC adopting and confirming the
TWG Detailed Bid Evaluation Report and declaring the
LCB;
• TWG Report on the Detailed Bid Evaluation for
determination of the bidder with the LCB;
• Resolutions of the BAC adopting and confirming the
TWG Post-Qualification Evaluation Report and
 declaring the LCRB;
• BAC Resolution Declaring LCRB and Recommending
approval by the HOPE; and
• Justification on the foregoing deviations.
13.9.3 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the BAC
to ensure that the issuance of Notice to Proceed to the successful
winning bidder is within seven calendar days from the date of
approval of the contract in compliance with Section 37.4.1 of the
2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in order to avoid delays in the
implementation of the contract.

13.10.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to
submit immediately the lacking documents and address the
deficiencies noted in post-audit to ensure the validity and propriety
of the abovementioned transactions.

14.5 We recommended that Management prioritize the implementation of
projects which are aligned to the prescribed usages of shares from tobacco
excise tax as provided under Section 2 of RA No. 7171 contributory to the
advancement of tobacco farmers.

We recommended that The Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly bank reconciliation statements pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 to ensure accuracy and to the reliability of the Cash in Bank- Local Currency, Current Account presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is continually locating the unreconciled difference.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant already preparing the monthly bank reconciliation statements and submitted the same to the Auditor. Hence, the discrepancy between the balance per book and balance per bank was decreased to the amount of P1,791,094.84 from the previous balance of P2,375,651.13. Continuing effort was exerted in locating said discrepancy.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that The Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to locate and revisit the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) entered into between the LGU and NGOs/POs for the specified mode of funds transferred to NGOs and POs for the specified mode of funds transferred the liquidation for the funds released and implement the same in order to recoup the funds which may be subsequently used by other constituents/ stakeholders for development purposes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has exerted her best effort to locate for the relevant records and MOAs for the MOAs entered into between the management and NGOS/POs. Results of which disclosed that these groups are still existing and there were collections received during the year. However, records for the receivable amounting to P200,000.00 from Gregorio del Pilar Furniture Association was not located. This has long been existing way back year 2010 and already inactive/dormant since no collection received since then. Further, verification of the receivables revealed the inclusion of long-outstanding accounts totaling P755,756.23 that have been existing since 2012 and which no records kept at the Accounting Office.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant acknowledge her inability to set up the allowance for impairment for the receivable that has no possibility of collection, but assured to prepare the adjusting journal entry this ensuing year. Moreover, verification of the receivables disclosed that the groups are still existing and there were collections received during the year; however, some records for certain receivables were not located.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Mayor require the Sanggunian Bayan to prepare an ordinance or resolution for the DBM’s approval to use for specific projects/programs the unexpended RA 7171 Congressional share of P1,500,000.00. Also, seek approval from the source agency on the re-appropriation of the unutilized fund for other development projects and programs of the LGU. Likewise, instruct the personnel concerned to locate/obtain and review the copy of MOA for the P422,492.00 to ascertain and implement the intended purpose/s for the benefit of the constituents. Moreover, instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer return the unexpended/unutilized balances of completed projects to the source agencies, thereby allowing them to use the same for other priority projects of the government.
observations.managementAction: Management already made an appropriation ordinance for the reappropriation of the amount of P1,500,000.00 during the year. The same was allotted and utilized and implemented. The Municipal Accountant claimed that there were no longer records kept for these liabilities. The Municipal Treasurer returned the total amount of P10,454.88 to the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.reason: The appropriation ordinance for the reappropriation of the amount of P1,500,000.00 was prepared and the amount was utilized for the Construction of Multi-Purpose Building Phase I at Poblacion Norte for implementation this ensuing year. However, verification of the financial statements revealed that amounts still amounted to P897,993.15 which implies that management were not able to return these amounts to donor agencies (GF- P169,837.52; TF- P728,155.63).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to trace the discrepancy between the balances of the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to reflect the correct balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already located said discrepancy and prepared adjusting journal entry to correct the same.
observations.reason: The discrepancy between the balances of the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds was located and corrected through adjusting journal entry to reflect the correct balances in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to: a. prepare and submit immediately the corresponding SWA, STEWA and detailed computation supporting the reported amount of liquidated damages per day to verify the correctness and accuracy of collected liquidated damages; b. submit immediately the corresponding Work Suspension/Work Resumption Orders and supporting documents or valid proofs of unworkable conditions such as weather reports, pictures and certification by the Punong Barangay to determine the reasonableness and validity of the claims for time suspensions; and c. adapt the calibrated actions in response to delays in the implementation of infrastructure projects provided in Item 7 of Annex E of the 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184 and Item 4.2 of GPPB Circular No. 03-2019.
observations.managementAction: Documents were submitted to the Auditor. Documents were submitted to the Auditor. The Municipal Engineer conducted close monitoring and supervision to ensure all ongoing projects for the year were completed before the expiration of the contract duration. We only noticed one project that was delayed in its completion but was supported with applicable Suspension and Resumption Orders.
observations.reason: The required SWA, STEWA and detailed computations and Work Suspension/Resumption Orders were submitted to the Auditor. We recommended that management:
a. through the Municipal Accountant require all pertinent documents before certifying the disbursement voucher as to the completeness of the supporting documents to avoid doubt on the validity and propriety of the transaction because of the deficiencies in the documentary requirements. Lacking documents must be submitted immediately to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension.
b. through the Municipal Accountant strictly enforce the withholding of at least one percent (1%) retention money of the total contract price from the payment made to the supplier or require a special bank guarantee before the release of the said amount after the lapse of the warranty period to assure that in case there will be defects/deficiencies from the deliveries, the supplier will be held responsible for correcting the said defects.
c. through the authorized inspector of the LGU conduct a careful and thorough check of the items delivered based on a duly approved Purchase Order with particular focus on the quality and quantity of the articles/items. Proper procedure in inspection will safeguard against attempts agency’s assets of supplier to defraud the government through wrong, short or substandard deliveries.
d. through the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) furnish the auditor copies of approved government contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents within five (5) working days upon perfection of the contract for review for the immediate communication of the defects and deficiencies of the contract. Also to avoid doubt on the validity of the contracts and to ensure the submission of all the necessary supporting documents before payment.
e. through the Municipal Accountant consider in the purchase of goods from a Non-VAT Registered supplier the withholding of the 3% Percentage Tax and 1% Income Tax. The person in-charge of withholding shall prepare the required forms and file the tax returns.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive designate a new committee to conduct physical inventory of all the Property, Plant and Equipment so as to determine their existence, correct valuation and serviceability. Likewise, the Inventory Report should be submitted to the auditor concerned for verification not later than January 31 of each year.

We recommended that management:
a. through the Municipal Accountant require all pertinent documents before certifying the disbursement voucher as to the completeness of the supporting documents to avoid doubt on the validity and propriety of the transaction because of the deficiencies in the documentary requirements. Lacking documents must be submitted immediately to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension.
b. through the Municipal Accountant strictly enforce the withholding of at least one percent (1%) retention money of the total contract price from the payment made to the supplier or require a special bank guarantee before the release of the said amount after the lapse of the warranty period to assure that in case there will be defects/deficiencies from the deliveries, the supplier will be held responsible for correcting the said defects.
c. through the authorized inspector of the LGU conduct a careful and thorough check of the items delivered based on a duly approved Purchase Order with particular focus on the quality and quantity of the articles/items. Proper procedure in inspection will safeguard agency’s assets against attempts of supplier to defraud the government through wrong, short or substandard deliveries.
d. through the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) furnish the auditor copies of approved government contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents within five (5) working days upon perfection of the contract for review for the immediate communication of the defects and deficiencies of the contract. Also to avoid doubt on the validity of the contracts and to ensure the submission of all the necessary supporting documents before payment.
e. through the Municipal Accountant consider in the purchase of goods from a Non-VAT Registered supplier the withholding of the 3% Percentage Tax and 1% Income Tax. The person in-charge of withholding shall prepare the required forms and file the tax returns.

We recommended that management avoid the incurrence of service charge arising from a returned check due to no sufficient funds balance. Service charge resulting from the negligence on the part of the officials having access and control on the financial affairs of the LGU should not be charged against the government funds. In addition, Funds received for the fulfillment of the purpose for which the trust was created should not be treated as idle funds and be placed in a time deposit account. All resources of the government shall be managed or utilized with efficiency, economy and effectiveness to avoid wastage of funds.

We recommended that management through the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the above cited provision. Submit on time all the necessary reports together with their complete supporting documents in order not to delay the post audit of transactions and preparation of other reports. It is the direct responsibility of the agency head to install, implement, and monitor sound internal control.

We recommended that management revert the unutilized balance of LDRRMF for 2012 to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund and shall be made available for other social services after subsequent enactment by the local sanggunian.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit monthly reconciliation statements in order to establish the accuracy and/or correctness of the Cash in Bank balances as required in COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended that Management through the Municipal Accountant strictly enforce the withholding of at least ten percent (10%) retention money of every progress billing based on the submitted Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA) to cover uncorrected discovered defects. The non-deduction of the 10% retention money exposed the Municipality to possible loss that may not be recovered due to uncorrected discovered defects and third party liabilities.

We recommended that management conduct the necessary field surveys and investigations in the detailed engineering and carefully study/evaluate the site condition in the preparation of accurate design plans, technical specifications, quantity and cost estimates and program of work for infrastructure projects to minimize any work suspensions that may cause delay in the completion of the projects.

We recommended management that, based on the foregoing provisions that additional compensation such as commissions, honoraria, fringe benefits, benefits in excess of the allowable statutory amount of P82,000.00 are not exempt from the mandatory Withholding of Income Tax on Compensation and to be remitted on or before the 10th day of the following month in which the withholding was made except for tax returns covering transactions in December which shall be filed on or before January 15 of the succeeding year.
